prerequisites: A Typeform account with access to the forms whose responses you want to extract. Register at typeform.com if you do not have one.

The form IDs of the typeforms you want to sync. A form ID is the alphanumeric string in the form's URL or admin panel; supply them in the optional form_ids field to limit the sync to specific forms (leave empty to attempt all accessible forms).
Credentials for one of the supported auth methods: either a personal access token, or an OAuth 2.0 application (client ID, client secret, and a refresh token).
At minimum the forms:read and responses:read permissions on the token so the connector can list forms and read their responses.
Optionally, a start_date (ISO 8601) to bound how far back response data is fetched.

authMethods.name: Personal access token (credentials.access_token)
authMethods.summary: Generate a personal access token from your Typeform account under the Account menu, Personal tokens, granting it at least the forms:read and responses:read scopes. Provide the token as credentials.access_token. Typeform passes it in the Authorization header to call the forms and responses APIs against https://api.typeform.com (or the EU base URL for EU-region accounts).

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 (credentials.client_id, credentials.client_secret, credentials.refresh_token)
authMethods.summary: Register an OAuth application under Organization, Developer Apps to obtain a client ID and client secret, and authorize it with at least forms:read, responses:read and the offline scope so Typeform issues a refresh token. Provide the client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token (plus access_token when available); the connector uses the refresh token to mint access tokens for API requests.

setupSteps.title: Choose an authentication method
setupSteps.body: Decide between a personal access token (simplest, tied to your own Typeform user) and an OAuth 2.0 application (uses a refresh token to obtain access tokens). Both populate the connector's credentials config field.

setupSteps.title: Create a personal access token (token method)
setupSteps.body: Log in to Typeform, open the dropdown menu next to your username and select Account, then click Personal tokens, then Generate a new token. Name the token and select the scopes it needs (include forms:read and responses:read). Click Generate token and copy the value, which looks like tfp_<hex>. Set it as credentials.access_token.

setupSteps.title: Register an OAuth application (OAuth method)
setupSteps.body: Log in to Typeform, open the dropdown next to your organization name, and under Organization choose Developer Apps, then Register a new app. Provide the app name, website URL, and redirect URI (use HTTPS; self-signed certificates are not accepted), then register the app. Copy the Client ID and the Client Secret (the secret is shown only once). Set these as credentials.client_id and credentials.client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Obtain an OAuth refresh token (OAuth method)
setupSteps.body: Run the OAuth authorization flow for your app, requesting at least the forms:read and responses:read scopes plus the offline scope so Typeform returns a refresh token. Store the resulting refresh token as credentials.refresh_token (and the access token as credentials.access_token if you have it).

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector source
setupSteps.body: Enter your credentials in the credentials field. Optionally set form_ids to the specific Typeform form IDs you want to sync, and set start_date (ISO 8601) to limit how far back responses are pulled. Leave the optional fields empty to use defaults.
